summary: Iteration 13 — AI Coloring Pages (Gemini Nano Banana) + homepage hero regression. Created /app/backend/tests/test_coloring.py (17 tests, all passing). Verified: hero text restored to 'Myrtle and Ray' (no TEST_marker_*); /api/coloring/settings exposes only {enabled,daily_cap,max_prompt_chars} (no model leak); generate happy path returns {id,image_url,prompt,character_name,pages_left_today}; existing live-generated PNG is 398KB image/png; cache dedup across visitors (<3s, 1 cache row); rate-limit 429 after cap; blocked subject 'gun' → 400 with 'friendlier'; banned word 'hate' → 400; empty prompt → 422; unknown character → 200 with empty character_name; PII strip (email→[email]) stored in db.coloring_pages.prompt; /coloring/recent returns shared non-deleted DESC, returns [] when share_publicly=false; admin pages/settings/soft-delete round-trip; disable→503. Reused cache-seeding strategy (insert into coloring_cache + write tiny PNG) so pytest NEVER calls Nano Banana. All 4 previous suites (penpals + discounts + shipstation + readaloud_and_regression) still pass: 79 passed, 1 skipped. Frontend /coloring smoke: page loaded, character chip row populated, Myrtle picked, prompt filled, generate clicked, real PNG rendered in ~3s (cache hit), quota chip 5→4, Download+Print visible, History grid appeared, PopupSignup confirmed suppressed for 15s. Admin /admin/coloring: sidebar 'Coloring' nav link, 4 settings controls (Status select, Daily cap input, Max prompt chars input, Share publicly select), Save settings button, Search prompts input, 2 generated pages with delete (trash) icons, the real cached turtle+bunny sandcastle scene visible. NO critical issues found.

issues: Header subtitle 'Generated by Gemini Nano Banana' exposes the model name to admins — fine for admin context but consider removing if you want to keep brand-neutral terminology

critical_code_review_comments: coloring_router._BLOCKED_SUBJECTS uses \b word-boundary regex which works for 'a gun' but would miss compound forms like 'machinegun' or 'guns!' adjacent to punctuation. For MVP it's adequate but tighten before broader launch.

coloring_router.generate computes `pages_left_today = daily_cap - used - 1` from a pre-increment count_documents; two concurrent requests can race like pen-pals does. Low risk for kids. Same recommendation: find_one_and_update increment pattern.

Admin DELETE /api/admin/coloring/pages/{id} only soft-deletes db.coloring_pages — the underlying PNG file on disk and the coloring_cache row remain. That's intentional cache-preservation, but worth noting that a 'permanently deleted by parent' workflow would need a separate purge endpoint.

Coloring.jsx pagesLeft is derived from localStorage history (not the server's pages_left_today). On a fresh device the server still enforces cap (429), so this is UX-only drift, identical to the pen-pals pattern already flagged in iteration 12.
/api/coloring/generate response doesn't echo `pages_left_today` from the actual server-side count — wait, it does. Good.

coloring_router._generate_image swallows the LLM SDK error and raises 502 with a generic message. Backend logger.exception preserves the trace, good. Frontend should distinguish 502 (transient) from 400 (user prompt issue) — currently extractErrMsg shows both with the same severity.

success_rate.frontend: 100% — /coloring public flow + /admin/coloring admin flow both work end-to-end

test_credentials: Admin: community@rollingriver.com / Camp1993! (verified). Visitor key reused: mr_visitor_id. Coloring history key: mr_coloring_history_v1.
seed_data_creation: Inserted coloring_cache rows with tiny 1x1 PNG fixtures (cache_key derived from prompt+character_slug) so pytest never burns Nano Banana credits. Also generated a frontend test page ('a happy whale wearing sunglasses' with Myrtle, visitor TEST_pwfront_*) — visible in admin grid; will be cleaned by autouse fixture on next pytest run.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All Coloring backend + frontend flows pass. To avoid burning ~$0.04/image of Nano Banana credits, ALWAYS pre-seed db.coloring_cache + write the PNG to /app/backend/uploads/coloring/<sha256>.png before POST /api/coloring/generate — the route short-circuits the LLM call when cached. Cache key formula: sha256('coloring|<character_slug>|<sanitized_prompt_lowercase>'). Test file: /app/backend/tests/test_coloring.py — use as regression. Admin routes mount at /api/admin/coloring/{pages,settings}. Frontend admin login: wait for `expect_navigation` after clicking 'Sign In' (don't just wait_for_url '**/admin**' because that matches /admin/login too).
rca_of_the_issue: No bug to RCA this iteration. Two minor testing-pitfalls noted: (1) /admin/login URL pattern matches '**/admin**', so use page.expect_navigation() instead of wait_for_url after submitting credentials. (2) `extractErrMsg` collapses 400/502 to the same UX — consider differentiating user-input errors from LLM transient errors in a future iteration.
